cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this problem. It's about a teacher and four students who are given numbers from a set S. The set S has four consecutive two-digit integers. Also, some number in S is divisible by 6, and a different number is divisible by 7. The students all say they can't figure out what S is initially, but after hearing that, they can. I need to find the sum of all possible values of the greatest element of S. First, let me recap the problem to make sure I understand. The set S has four consecutive two-digit numbers. Each student gets one number from S, all different. The teacher tells them that in S, there's one divisible by 6 and another by 7. Then the teacher asks if they can figure out S. All four say no.
